title: graphephorum muticum
intro: Graphephorum muticum, also known as Licorice Root or Licorice Fern, is a perennial plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Graphephorum muticum include reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues, as well as providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Graphephorum muticum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the roots or rhizomes in hot water or a solvent such as ethanol.
